Raizza Mae G. Barza

Program: BS Electrical Engineering

Course: EE 417 - Seminars and Colloquia

School: Batangas State University - The National Engineering University - Alangilan Campus

Instructor: Engr. Elenor M. Reyes, PEE

Timeline of Events

Seminar Journey

Browse the complete seminar and colloquia series from February to May 2026. Use the search and filters to quickly find topics, event modes, and key learning points.

UplEEft

Empowering the Future - Human and Technical Skills, One Engineer

🏛️ In-person
📅 February 20, 2026 ⏱️ 1:00 PM - 4:00 PM 📍 Amphitheater, Albert Einstein Building, Batangas State University - Alangilan Campus

Overview: The seminar focused on developing future engineers by strengthening both technical knowledge and professional character. It connected classroom concepts to real industry settings, especially in system monitoring, safety practices, renewable energy compliance, and professional growth.

Key Takeaway: Engineering growth depends on technical ability and personal discipline. SCADA highlighted real-time monitoring and control systems, while the Renewable Energy Act emphasized policies, net metering, renewable energy standards, and grid interconnection. The event also reinforced responsibility, resilience, teamwork, and communication.

  • SCADA
  • Personal Development
  • Renewable Energy Act
View Event Poster
View Proof of Attendance

ENGtrained

Foundational and Emerging Research Directions in Engineering

💻 Online
📅 March 12, 2026 ⏱️ 1:00 PM - 4:00 PM 📍 Zoom

Overview: This online colloquium presented engineering research studies addressing practical problems in electrical systems, automation, sustainable infrastructure, battery charging, and laboratory applications.

Key Takeaway: The event showed that research is a practical tool for safer distribution systems, improved automation, sustainable building design, efficient charging technology, and better laboratory learning applications.

  • Underground electrical distribution layout for Balayan, Batangas
  • Automation in electrical system and illumination design for an adaptive sports-cum-convention center
  • Automated system for cornhusk fiber extractor and cordage making
  • Ultra-fast charging system for an electric bicycle battery
  • Smart and sustainable electrical infrastructure for hotel and resort architecture
  • Schneider PLC laboratory activity for an automated water supply system
View Event Poster
View Proof of Attendance

Frontiers

Foraging the Forefront of Engineering and Resilience

💻 Online
📅 March 27, 2026 ⏱️ 2:00 PM - 5:00 PM 📍 Zoom

Overview: This panel discussion explored the connection between engineering practice, maintenance and operation, innovation, and mental resilience. It emphasized professionalism under pressure and responsible decision-making.

Key Takeaway: Engineering requires resilience, patience, flexibility, and pressure management, especially in field operations and maintenance. Engineers must communicate properly, make responsible decisions, and remain committed during challenges.

  • Engineering Maintenance and Operation
  • Innovation in Engineering Practice
  • Mental Resilience
  • Professional Challenges in the Engineering Field
  • Technical Skills and Emotional Preparedness
  • Adaptability, Responsibility, and Communication in Engineering Work
View Event Poster
View Proof of Attendance

Frontiers 2.0

Foraging the Forefront of Electrical Engineering

💻 Online
📅 April 1, 2026 ⏱️ 8:00 AM - 11:00 AM 📍 Zoom

Overview: The event focused on key challenges, innovations, and practical applications in electrical engineering, including voltage drop calculations, high-rise building design, and Energy Management Systems.

Key Takeaway: The event emphasized precision and proper analysis in electrical design. Accurate voltage drop calculations, careful load planning, and energy monitoring all contribute to safer and more efficient systems.

  • Voltage Drop Calculation for Low, Medium, and High Voltage Settings
  • Electrical Design Analysis for Mixed-Use High-Rise Building Design
  • Introduction to Energy Management Systems
  • Electrical System Efficiency
  • Design Evaluation and Practical Engineering Applications
View Event Poster
View Proof of Attendance

ElecSynapse

Forum on Electrical Engineering Innovation and Emerging Technologies

💻 Online
📅 April 10, 2026 ⏱️ 1:00 PM - 4:00 PM 📍 Zoom

Overview: This forum presented innovative engineering projects applying automation, renewable energy, smart technology, and electrical system design to real-world problems.

Key Takeaway: Emerging technologies can support automation, energy efficiency, renewable energy use, smart monitoring, sustainable lighting, and safer infrastructure design.

  • Automated sugarcane cutter with adaptive length, bud viability sensing, and seed counting
  • Web-based retention monitoring and prediction system using BSEE graduate cohorts
  • SNIP: Smart Energy Plug
  • Dual-axis rack-mounted heliostat mirror solar tracking system
  • Counter-rotating vertical wind turbine integrated into streetlamps
  • Underground cabling design of Tinga-Sampaga Bypass Road
View Event Poster
View Proof of Attendance

Eureeka

Innovating Ideas to Electrical Engineering Research

💻 Online
📅 April 24, 2026 ⏱️ 1:00 PM - 4:00 PM 📍 Zoom

Overview: This colloquium featured research concepts involving electrical automation, energy optimization, localized government automation, automated machines, and small-scale renewable power generation.

Key Takeaway: Electrical engineering research can improve public services, community systems, automation, energy optimization, renewable power, and practical solutions for real-world needs.

  • Focus Point Study Hub
  • Electrical automation for Innoheritage: Museum, Culture and Arts Building, and Innovation Hub
  • Automated Bottle Processing and Valuation Machine
  • Dynamic multi-source power transfer and synchronization control with energy optimization
  • iBarangay platform for localized government automation
  • Vortex-hydro turbine generator for small-scale power generation in Bauan, Batangas
View Event Poster
View Proof of Attendance

Nikolas

Next-Generation Innovators Electrical Engineering Systems

💻 Online
📅 May 8, 2026 ⏱️ 1:00 PM - 4:30 PM 📍 Zoom

Overview: This online program presented research projects focused on renewable energy, automation, energy management, educational simulation, and sustainable electrical system design.

Key Takeaway: Electrical engineering can support sustainability in homes, schools, agriculture, and community spaces through solar-powered systems, automation, energy management, and green building design.

  • Solardry Retract: solar-powered automated retractable clothesline
  • Automated dual output packaging machine for small-scale black pepper production
  • Gamified simulation for foundational pneumatics and electropneumatics education
  • Mobile solar-powered system for energy and water resilience in Batangas Province
  • Solar concrete walkway
  • Electrical system assessment and energy management proposal for Rosario East Central School
  • Electrical system design for Enseñando Lipeño with green and modern architecture
View Event Poster
View Proof of Attendance

EEHIVE

Electrical Engineering Innovators Conference 2026

💻 Online
📅 May 15, 2026 ⏱️ 1:00 PM - 4:30 PM 📍 Zoom

Overview: This online student research conference showcased studies in agriculture, renewable energy, power systems, electronics, simulation, and automation.

Key Takeaway: The conference served as a strong conclusion to the series by showing how student research can generate practical, sustainable, and relevant solutions for communities and industries.

  • Automated coir pot production Project A: coir feeder, rollers, needle felting, and binding-agent coater process
  • Automated coir pot production Project B: heat press molding, trimming, and ejection process
  • Solar-powered drip irrigation system for dragon fruit cultivation
  • IoT-enabled adaptive microcontroller-based three-phase power factor correction device
  • Direct-current electroculture techniques applied to hydroponics
  • Web-based power flow simulator
  • SPARTAS: solar-powered automated rodent trap and alarm system
View Event Poster
View Proof of Attendance

Synthesis and Reflection

Overall Learning

A concise conclusion based on the complete seminar and colloquia series.

Synthesis

The seminar and colloquia series presented the broad scope of electrical engineering, from technical systems and renewable energy to research innovation, automation, sustainability, and professional development. The events showed that engineering is not limited to theories and calculations, but is strongly connected to solving real-world problems in communities, industries, buildings, agriculture, power systems, and public service.

Overall Reflection

Overall, these events gave me a deeper appreciation of electrical engineering as a field that evolves with technology, innovation, and society. They inspired me to continue developing technical skills, research ability, and personal character so I can become a future engineer capable of creating practical, sustainable, and meaningful solutions.
